name: BELOW ZERO | CAP-29
description:
BELOW ZERO

In 2029, a fungal infection known as CAP-29 began spreading across the globe. The established order collapsed, leaving behind only the ashes of civilization, while chaos and madness became the new pillars of this mangled world.

Below Zero is a chronicle of how the fungal plague consumed Russia, permanently erasing its former identity from history.

When the contagion surged across the borders, the country was unprepared for a strike of such magnitude. In 2031, a tragedy occurred that is now known as The Collapse of Russia. Republics and regions, once unified, shattered into dozens of warring factions. But in the fire of internecine wars and under the onslaught of endless waves of the infected, these shards of the empire extinguished one by one.

Millions of refugees headed east, hoping to find salvation in the forests of Siberia. But the harsh Ural Mountains became an insurmountable barrier for most. In 2034, among these snow-capped ridges, Nikolai Yuryevich Novikov founded the Ural Defense Corps (UDC). Cruel, totalitarian, and ruthless — it became the only force capable of curbing the infection and pushing back the advancing chaos.

However, in 2036, a new disaster struck. The fungus evolved, overcoming the temperature barrier and transforming into the CAP-29/F strain. Now, the cold does not protect — it only hides the danger.

A heavy and bleak adventure awaits you in the frozen taiga of the Ural region. A place where six unique and tragic destinies try to survive, balancing on the edge of madness, and preserve the last remnants of humanity in a world where the very sky smells of death.

CAP-29/F

Until 2036, winter was humanity's only ally. The frost shackled the mycelium, turning the infected into brittle ice statues, incapable of hunting. But nature found a way to overcome this barrier, making a gruesome evolutionary leap in the snow-covered forests of the Urals. Thus was born the CAP-29/F strain—the "Ural" variant of death.

Ice Immunity The fungus learned to convert the host's blood and lymph into biological antifreeze. By synthesizing glycerin and specific proteins, the pathogen prevents ice crystals from destroying cell membranes. Where ordinary flesh turns to stone, tissues permeated with CAP-29/F threads maintain elasticity even at temperatures of -40°C. The cold no longer kills the fungus—it serves as its natural habitat.

Biological Furnace The most terrifying man
creator: DragoRUS
